verses.sanskrit: मैत्रेय उवाचचरम: सद्विशेषाणामनेकोऽसंयुत: सदा ।परमाणु: स विज्ञेयो नृणामैक्यभ्रमो यत: ॥ १ ॥
verses.transliteration: maitreya uvācacaramaḥ sad-viśeṣāṇāmaneko ’saṁyutaḥ sadāparamāṇuḥ sa vijñeyonṛṇām aikya-bhramo yataḥ
verses.text: The material manifestation’s ultimate particle, which is indivisible and not formed into a body, is called the atom. It exists always as an invisible identity, even after the dissolution of all forms. The material body is but a combination of such atoms, but it is misunderstood by the common man.

verses.sanskrit: सत एव पदार्थस्य स्वरूपावस्थितस्य यत् ।कैवल्यं परममहानविशेषो निरन्तर: ॥ २ ॥
verses.transliteration: sata eva padārthasyasvarūpāvasthitasya yatkaivalyaṁ parama-mahānaviśeṣo nirantaraḥ
verses.text: Atoms are the ultimate state of the manifest universe. When they stay in their own forms without forming different bodies, they are called the unlimited oneness. There are certainly different bodies in physical forms, but the atoms themselves form the complete manifestation.

verses.sanskrit: एवं कालोऽप्यनुमित: सौक्ष्म्ये स्थौल्ये च सत्तम ।संस्थानभुक्त्या भगवानव्यक्तो व्यक्तभुग्विभु: ॥ ३ ॥
verses.transliteration: evaṁ kālo ’py anumitaḥsaukṣmye sthaulye ca sattamasaṁsthāna-bhuktyā bhagavānavyakto vyakta-bhug vibhuḥ
verses.text: One can estimate time by measuring the movement of the atomic combination of bodies. Time is the potency of the almighty Personality of Godhead, Hari, who controls all physical movement although He is not visible in the physical world.

verses.sanskrit: स काल: परमाणुर्वै यो भुङ्क्ते परमाणुताम् ।सतोऽविशेषभुग्यस्तु स काल: परमो महान् ॥ ४ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa kālaḥ paramāṇur vaiyo bhuṅkte paramāṇutāmsato ’viśeṣa-bhug yas tusa kālaḥ paramo mahān
verses.text: Atomic time is measured according to its covering a particular atomic space. That time which covers the unmanifest aggregate of atoms is called the great time.

verses.sanskrit: अणुर्द्वौ परमाणु स्यात्त्रसरेणुस्त्रय: स्मृत: ।जालार्करश्म्यवगत: खमेवानुपतन्नगात् ॥ ५ ॥
verses.transliteration: aṇur dvau paramāṇū syāttrasareṇus trayaḥ smṛtaḥjālārka-raśmy-avagataḥkham evānupatann agāt
verses.text: The division of gross time is calculated as follows: two atoms make one double atom, and three double atoms make one hexatom. This hexatom is visible in the sunshine which enters through the holes of a window screen. One can clearly see that the hexatom goes up towards the sky.

verses.sanskrit: त्रसरेणुत्रिकं भुङ्क्ते य: काल: स त्रुटि: स्मृत: ।शतभागस्तु वेध: स्यात्तैस्त्रिभिस्तु लव: स्मृत: ॥ ६ ॥
verses.transliteration: trasareṇu-trikaṁ bhuṅkteyaḥ kālaḥ sa truṭiḥ smṛtaḥśata-bhāgas tu vedhaḥ syāttais tribhis tu lavaḥ smṛtaḥ
verses.text: The time duration needed for the integration of three trasareṇus is called a truṭi, and one hundred truṭis make one vedha. Three vedhas make one lava.

verses.sanskrit: निमेषस्त्रिलवो ज्ञेय आम्नातस्ते त्रय: क्षण: ।क्षणान् पञ्च विदु: काष्ठां लघु ता दश पञ्च च ॥ ७ ॥
verses.transliteration: nimeṣas tri-lavo jñeyaāmnātas te trayaḥ kṣaṇaḥkṣaṇān pañca viduḥ kāṣṭhāṁlaghu tā daśa pañca ca
verses.text: The duration of time of three lavas is equal to one nimeṣa, the combination of three nimeṣas makes one kṣaṇa, five kṣaṇas combined together make one kāṣṭhā, and fifteen kāṣṭhās make one laghu.

verses.sanskrit: लघूनि वै समाम्नाता दश पञ्च च नाडिका ।ते द्वे मुहूर्त: प्रहर: षड्याम: सप्त नृणाम् ॥ ८ ॥
verses.transliteration: laghūni vai samāmnātādaśa pañca ca nāḍikāte dve muhūrtaḥ praharaḥṣaḍ yāmaḥ sapta vā nṛṇām
verses.text: Fifteen laghus make one nāḍikā, which is also called a daṇḍa. Two daṇḍas make one muhūrta, and six or seven daṇḍas make one fourth of a day or night, according to human calculation.

verses.sanskrit: द्वादशार्धपलोन्मानं चतुर्भिश्चतुरङ्गुलै: ।स्वर्णमाषै: कृतच्छिद्रं यावत्प्रस्थजलप्लुतम् ॥ ९ ॥
verses.transliteration: dvādaśārdha-palonmānaṁcaturbhiś catur-aṅgulaiḥsvarṇa-māṣaiḥ kṛta-cchidraṁyāvat prastha-jala-plutam
verses.text: The measuring pot for one nāḍikā, or daṇḍa, can be prepared with a six-pala-weight [fourteen ounce] pot of copper, in which a hole is bored with a gold probe weighing four māṣa and measuring four fingers long. When the pot is placed on water, the time before the water overflows in the pot is called one daṇḍa.

verses.sanskrit: यामाश्चत्वारश्चत्वारो मर्त्यानामहनी उभे ।पक्ष: पञ्चदशाहानि शुक्ल: कृष्णश्च मानद ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: yāmāś catvāraś catvāromartyānām ahanī ubhepakṣaḥ pañca-daśāhāniśuklaḥ kṛṣṇaś ca mānada
verses.text: It is calculated that there are four praharas, which are also called yāmas, in the day and four in the night of the human being. Similarly, fifteen days and nights are a fortnight, and there are two fortnights, white and black, in a month.

verses.sanskrit: तयो: समुच्चयो मास: पितृणां तदहर्निशम् ।द्वौ तावृतु: षडयनं दक्षिणं चोत्तरं दिवि ॥ ११ ॥
verses.transliteration: tayoḥ samuccayo māsaḥpitṝṇāṁ tad ahar-niśamdvau tāv ṛtuḥ ṣaḍ ayanaṁdakṣiṇaṁ cottaraṁ divi
verses.text: The aggregate of two fortnights is one month, and that period is one complete day and night for the Pitā planets. Two of such months comprise one season, and six months comprise one complete movement of the sun from south to north.

verses.sanskrit: अयने चाहनी प्राहुर्वत्सरो द्वादश स्मृत: ।संवत्सरशतं नृणांपरमायुर्निरूपितम् ॥ १२ ॥
verses.transliteration: ayane cāhanī prāhurvatsaro dvādaśa smṛtaḥsaṁvatsara-śataṁ nṝṇāṁparamāyur nirūpitam
verses.text: Two solar movements make one day and night of the demigods, and that combination of day and night is one complete calendar year for the human being. The human being has a duration of life of one hundred years.

verses.sanskrit: ग्रहर्क्षताराचक्रस्थ: परमाण्वादिना जगत् ।संवत्सरावसानेन पर्येत्यनिमिषो विभु: ॥ १३ ॥
verses.transliteration: graharkṣa-tārā-cakra-sthaḥparamāṇv-ādinā jagatsaṁvatsarāvasānenaparyety animiṣo vibhuḥ
verses.text: Influential stars, planets, luminaries and atoms all over the universe are rotating in their respective orbits under the direction of the Supreme, represented by eternal kāla.

verses.sanskrit: संवत्सर: परिवत्सर इडावत्सर एव च ।अनुवत्सरो वत्सरश्च विदुरैवं प्रभाष्यते ॥ १४ ॥
verses.transliteration: saṁvatsaraḥ parivatsaraiḍā-vatsara eva caanuvatsaro vatsaraś caviduraivaṁ prabhāṣyate
verses.text: There are five different names for the orbits of the sun, moon, stars and luminaries in the firmament, and they each have their own saṁvatsara.

verses.sanskrit: य: सृज्यशक्तिमुरुधोच्छ्‌वसयन् स्वशक्त्यापुंसोऽभ्रमाय दिवि धावति भूतभेद: ।कालाख्यया गुणमयं क्रतुभिर्वितन्वं-स्तस्मै बलिं हरत वत्सरपञ्चकाय ॥ १५ ॥
verses.transliteration: yaḥ sṛjya-śaktim urudhocchvasayan sva-śaktyāpuṁso ’bhramāya divi dhāvati bhūta-bhedaḥkālākhyayā guṇamayaṁ kratubhir vitanvaṁstasmai baliṁ harata vatsara-pañcakāya
verses.text: O Vidura, the sun enlivens all living entities with his unlimited heat and light. He diminishes the duration of life of all living entities in order to release them from their illusion of material attachment, and he enlarges the path of elevation to the heavenly kingdom. He thus moves in the firmament with great velocity, and therefore everyone should offer him respects once every five years with all ingredients of worship.

verses.sanskrit: विदुर उवाचपितृदेवमनुष्याणामायु: परमिदं स्मृतम् ।परेषां गतिमाचक्ष्व ये स्यु:कल्पाद् बहिर्विद: ॥ १६ ॥
verses.transliteration: vidura uvācapitṛ-deva-manuṣyāṇāmāyuḥ param idaṁ smṛtampareṣāṁ gatim ācakṣvaye syuḥ kalpād bahir vidaḥ
verses.text: Vidura said: I now understand the life durations of the residents of the Pitā planets and heavenly planets as well as that of the human beings. Now kindly inform me of the durations of life of those greatly learned living entities who are beyond the range of a kalpa.

verses.sanskrit: भगवान् वेद कालस्य गतिं भगवतो ननु ।विश्वं विचक्षते धीरा योगराद्धेन चक्षुषा ॥ १७ ॥
verses.transliteration: bhagavān veda kālasyagatiṁ bhagavato nanuviśvaṁ vicakṣate dhīrāyoga-rāddhena cakṣuṣā
verses.text: O spiritually powerful one, you can understand the movements of eternal time, which is the controlling form of the Supreme Personality of Godhead. Because you are a self-realized person, you can see everything by the power of mystic vision.

verses.sanskrit: मैत्रेय उवाचकृतं त्रेता द्वापरं च कलिश्चेति चतुर्युगम् ।दिव्यैर्द्वादशभिर्वर्षै: सावधानं निरूपितम् ॥ १८ ॥
verses.transliteration: maitreya uvācakṛtaṁ tretā dvāparaṁ cakaliś ceti catur-yugamdivyair dvādaśabhir varṣaiḥsāvadhānaṁ nirūpitam
verses.text: Maitreya said: O Vidura, the four millenniums are called the Satya-, Tretā-, Dvāpara- and Kali-yuga. The aggregate number of years of all of these combined is equal to twelve thousand years of the demigods.

verses.sanskrit: चत्वारि त्रीणि द्वै चैकं कृतादिषु यथाक्रमम् ।संख्यातानि सहस्राणि द्विगुणानि शतानि च ॥ १९ ॥
verses.transliteration: catvāri trīṇi dve caikaṁkṛtādiṣu yathā-kramamsaṅkhyātāni sahasrāṇidvi-guṇāni śatāni ca
verses.text: The duration of the Satya millennium equals 4,800 years of the years of the demigods; the duration of the Tretā millennium equals 3,600 years of the demigods; the duration of the Dvāpara millennium equals 2,400 years; and that of the Kali millennium is 1,200 years of the demigods.

verses.sanskrit: सन्ध्यासन्ध्यांशयोरन्तर्य: काल: शतसंख्ययो: ।तमेवाहुर्युगं तज्ज्ञा यत्र धर्मो विधीयते ॥ २० ॥
verses.transliteration: sandhyā-sandhyāṁśayor antaryaḥ kālaḥ śata-saṅkhyayoḥtam evāhur yugaṁ taj-jñāyatra dharmo vidhīyate
verses.text: The transitional periods before and after every millennium, which are a few hundred years as aforementioned, are known as yuga-sandhyās, or the conjunctions of two millenniums, according to the expert astronomers. In those periods all kinds of religious activities are performed.

verses.sanskrit: धर्मश्चतुष्पान्मनुजान् कृते समनुवर्तते ।स एवान्येष्वधर्मेण व्येति पादेन वर्धता ॥ २१ ॥
verses.transliteration: dharmaś catuṣ-pān manujānkṛte samanuvartatesa evānyeṣv adharmeṇavyeti pādena vardhatā
verses.text: O Vidura, in the Satya millennium mankind properly and completely maintained the principles of religion, but in other millenniums religion gradually decreased by one part as irreligion was proportionately admitted.

verses.sanskrit: त्रिलोक्या युगसाहस्रं बहिराब्रह्मणो दिनम् ।तावत्येव निशा तात यन्निमीलति विश्वसृक् ॥ २२ ॥
verses.transliteration: tri-lokyā yuga-sāhasraṁbahir ābrahmaṇo dinamtāvaty eva niśā tātayan nimīlati viśva-sṛk
verses.text: Outside of the three planetary systems [Svarga, Martya and Pātāla], the four yugas multiplied by one thousand comprise one day on the planet of Brahmā. A similar period comprises a night of Brahmā, in which the creator of the universe goes to sleep.

verses.sanskrit: निशावसान आरब्धो लोककल्पोऽनुवर्तते ।यावद्दिनं भगवतो मनून् भुञ्जंश्चतुर्दश ॥ २३ ॥
verses.transliteration: niśāvasāna ārabdholoka-kalpo ’nuvartateyāvad dinaṁ bhagavatomanūn bhuñjaṁś catur-daśa
verses.text: After the end of Brahmā’s night, the creation of the three worlds begins again in the daytime of Brahmā, and they continue to exist through the life durations of fourteen consecutive Manus, or fathers of mankind.

verses.sanskrit: स्वं स्वं कालं मनुर्भुङ्क्ते साधिकां ह्येकसप्ततिम् ॥ २४ ॥
verses.transliteration: svaṁ svaṁ kālaṁ manur bhuṅktesādhikāṁ hy eka-saptatim
verses.text: Each and every Manu enjoys a life of a little more than seventy-one sets of four millenniums.

verses.sanskrit: मन्वन्तरेषु मनवस्तद्वंश्या ऋषय: सुरा: ।भवन्ति चैव युगपत्सुरेशाश्चानु ये च तान् ॥ २५ ॥
verses.transliteration: manvantareṣu manavastad-vaṁśyā ṛṣayaḥ surāḥbhavanti caiva yugapatsureśāś cānu ye ca tān
verses.text: After the dissolution of each and every Manu, the next Manu comes in order, along with his descendants, who rule over the different planets; but the seven famous sages, and demigods like Indra and their followers, such as the Gandharvas, all appear simultaneously with Manu.

verses.sanskrit: एष दैनन्दिन: सर्गो ब्राह्मस्त्रैलोक्यवर्तन: ।तिर्यङ्‌नृपितृदेवानां सम्भवो यत्र कर्मभि: ॥ २६ ॥
verses.transliteration: eṣa dainan-dinaḥ sargobrāhmas trailokya-vartanaḥtiryaṅ-nṛ-pitṛ-devānāṁsambhavo yatra karmabhiḥ
verses.text: In the creation, during Brahmā’s day, the three planetary systems — Svarga, Martya and Pātāla — revolve, and the inhabitants, including the lower animals, human beings, demigods and Pitās, appear and disappear in terms of their fruitive activities.

verses.sanskrit: मन्वन्तरेषु भगवान् बिभ्रत्सत्त्वं स्वमूर्तिभि: ।मन्वादिभिरिदं विश्वमवत्युदितपौरुष: ॥ २७ ॥
verses.transliteration: manvantareṣu bhagavānbibhrat sattvaṁ sva-mūrtibhiḥmanv-ādibhir idaṁ viśvamavaty udita-pauruṣaḥ
verses.text: In each and every change of Manu, the Supreme Personality of Godhead appears by manifesting His internal potency in different incarnations, as Manu and others. Thus He maintains the universe by discovered power.

verses.sanskrit: तमोमात्रामुपादाय प्रतिसंरुद्धविक्रम: ।कालेनानुगताशेष आस्ते तूष्णीं दिनात्यये ॥ २८ ॥
verses.transliteration: tamo-mātrām upādāyapratisaṁruddha-vikramaḥkālenānugatāśeṣaāste tūṣṇīṁ dinātyaye
verses.text: At the end of the day, under the insignificant portion of the mode of darkness, the powerful manifestation of the universe merges in the darkness of night. By the influence of eternal time, the innumerable living entities remain merged in that dissolution, and everything is silent.

verses.sanskrit: तमेवान्वपिधीयन्ते लोको भूरादयस्त्रय: ।निशायामनुवृत्तायां निर्मुक्तशशिभास्करम् ॥ २९ ॥
verses.transliteration: tam evānv api dhīyantelokā bhūr-ādayas trayaḥniśāyām anuvṛttāyāṁnirmukta-śaśi-bhāskaram
verses.text: When the night of Brahmā ensues, all the three worlds are out of sight, and the sun and the moon are without glare, just as in the due course of an ordinary night.

verses.sanskrit: त्रिलोक्यां दह्यमानायां शक्त्या सङ्कर्षणाग्निना ।यान्त्यूष्मणा महर्लोकाज्जनं भृग्वादयोऽर्दिता: ॥ ३० ॥
verses.transliteration: tri-lokyāṁ dahyamānāyāṁśaktyā saṅkarṣaṇāgnināyānty ūṣmaṇā maharlokājjanaṁ bhṛgv-ādayo ’rditāḥ
verses.text: The devastation takes place due to the fire emanating from the mouth of Saṅkarṣaṇa, and thus great sages like Bhṛgu and other inhabitants of Maharloka transport themselves to Janaloka, being distressed by the warmth of the blazing fire which rages through the three worlds below.

verses.sanskrit: तावत्‍त्रिभुवनं सद्य: कल्पान्तैधितसिन्धव: ।प्लावयन्त्युत्कटाटोपचण्डवातेरितोर्मय: ॥ ३१ ॥
verses.transliteration: tāvat tri-bhuvanaṁ sadyaḥkalpāntaidhita-sindhavaḥplāvayanty utkaṭāṭopa-caṇḍa-vāteritormayaḥ
verses.text: At the beginning of the devastation all the seas overflow, and hurricane winds blow very violently. Thus the waves of the seas become ferocious, and in no time at all the three worlds are full of water.

verses.sanskrit: अन्त: स तस्मिन् सलिल आस्तेऽनन्तासनो हरि: ।योगनिद्रानिमीलाक्ष: स्तूयमानो जनालयै: ॥ ३२ ॥
verses.transliteration: antaḥ sa tasmin salilaāste ’nantāsano hariḥyoga-nidrā-nimīlākṣaḥstūyamāno janālayaiḥ
verses.text: The Supreme Lord, the Personality of Godhead, lies down in the water on the seat of Ananta, with His eyes closed, and the inhabitants of the Janaloka planets offer unto the Lord their glorious prayers with folded hands.

verses.sanskrit: एवंविधैरहोरात्रै: कालगत्योपलक्षितै: ।अपक्षितमिवास्यापि परमायुर्वय: शतम् ॥ ३३ ॥
verses.transliteration: evaṁ-vidhair aho-rātraiḥkāla-gatyopalakṣitaiḥapakṣitam ivāsyāpiparamāyur vayaḥ-śatam
verses.text: Thus the process of the exhaustion of the duration of life exists for every one of the living beings, including Lord Brahmā. One’s life endures for only one hundred years, in terms of the times in the different planets.

verses.sanskrit: यदर्धमायुषस्तस्य परार्धमभिधीयते ।पूर्व: परार्धोऽपक्रान्तो ह्यपरोऽद्य प्रवर्तते ॥ ३४ ॥
verses.transliteration: yad ardham āyuṣas tasyaparārdham abhidhīyatepūrvaḥ parārdho ’pakrāntohy aparo ’dya pravartate
verses.text: The one hundred years of Brahmā’s life are divided into two parts, the first half and the second half. The first half of the duration of Brahmā’s life is already over, and the second half is now current.

verses.sanskrit: पूर्वस्यादौ परार्धस्य ब्राह्मो नाम महानभूत् ।कल्पो यत्राभवद्ब्रह्मा शब्दब्रह्मेति यं विदु: ॥ ३५ ॥
verses.transliteration: pūrvasyādau parārdhasyabrāhmo nāma mahān abhūtkalpo yatrābhavad brahmāśabda-brahmeti yaṁ viduḥ
verses.text: In the beginning of the first half of Brahmā’s life, there was a millennium called Brāhma-kalpa, wherein Lord Brahmā appeared. The birth of the Vedas was simultaneous with Brahmā’s birth.

verses.sanskrit: तस्यैव चान्ते कल्पोऽभूद् यं पाद्ममभिचक्षते ।यद्धरेर्नाभिसरस आसील्लोकसरोरुहम् ॥ ३६ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasyaiva cānte kalpo ’bhūdyaṁ pādmam abhicakṣateyad dharer nābhi-sarasaāsīl loka-saroruham
verses.text: The millennium which followed the first Brāhma millennium is known as the Pādma-kalpa because in that millennium the universal lotus flower grew out of the navel reservoir of water of the Personality of Godhead, Hari.

verses.sanskrit: अयं तु कथित: कल्पो द्वितीयस्यापि भारत ।वाराह इति विख्यातो यत्रासीच्छूकरो हरि: ॥ ३७ ॥
verses.transliteration: ayaṁ tu kathitaḥ kalpodvitīyasyāpi bhāratavārāha iti vikhyātoyatrāsīc chūkaro hariḥ
verses.text: O descendant of Bharata, the first millennium in the second half of the life of Brahmā is also known as the Vārāha millennium because the Personality of Godhead appeared in that millennium as the hog incarnation.

verses.sanskrit: कालोऽयं द्विपरार्धाख्यो निमेष उपचर्यते ।अव्याकृतस्यानन्तस्य ह्यनादेर्जगदात्मन: ॥ ३८ ॥
verses.transliteration: kālo ’yaṁ dvi-parārdhākhyonimeṣa upacaryateavyākṛtasyānantasyahy anāder jagad-ātmanaḥ
verses.text: The duration of the two parts of Brahmā’s life, as above mentioned, is calculated to be equal to one nimeṣa [less than a second] for the Supreme Personality of Godhead, who is unchanging and unlimited and is the cause of all causes of the universe.

verses.sanskrit: कालोऽयं परमाण्वादिर्द्विपरार्धान्त ईश्वर: ।नैवेशितुं प्रभुर्भूम्न ईश्वरो धाममानिनाम् ॥ ३९ ॥
verses.transliteration: kālo ’yaṁ paramāṇv-ādirdvi-parārdhānta īśvaraḥnaiveśituṁ prabhur bhūmnaīśvaro dhāma-māninām
verses.text: Eternal time is certainly the controller of different dimensions, from that of the atom up to the superdivisions of the duration of Brahmā’s life; but, nevertheless, it is controlled by the Supreme. Time can control only those who are body conscious, even up to the Satyaloka or the other higher planets of the universe.

verses.sanskrit: विकारै: सहितो युक्तैर्विशेषादिभिरावृत: ।आण्डकोशो बहिरयं पञ्चाशत्कोटिविस्तृत: ॥ ४० ॥
verses.transliteration: vikāraiḥ sahito yuktairviśeṣādibhir āvṛtaḥāṇḍakośo bahir ayaṁpañcāśat-koṭi-vistṛtaḥ
verses.text: This phenomenal material world is expanded to a diameter of four billion miles, as a combination of eight material elements transformed into sixteen further categories, within and without, as follows.

verses.sanskrit: दशोत्तराधिकैर्यत्र प्रविष्ट: परमाणुवत् ।लक्ष्यतेऽन्तर्गताश्चान्ये कोटिशो ह्यण्डराशय: ॥ ४१ ॥
verses.transliteration: daśottarādhikair yatrapraviṣṭaḥ paramāṇuvatlakṣyate ’ntar-gatāś cānyekoṭiśo hy aṇḍa-rāśayaḥ
verses.text: The layers or elements covering the universes are each ten times thicker than the one before, and all the universes clustered together appear like atoms in a huge combination.

verses.sanskrit: तदाहुरक्षरं ब्रह्म सर्वकारणकारणम् ।विष्णोर्धाम परं साक्षात्पुरुषस्य महात्मन: ॥ ४२ ॥
verses.transliteration: tad āhur akṣaraṁ brahmasarva-kāraṇa-kāraṇamviṣṇor dhāma paraṁ sākṣātpuruṣasya mahātmanaḥ
verses.text: The Supreme Personality of Godhead, Śrī Kṛṣṇa, is therefore said to be the original cause of all causes. Thus the spiritual abode of Viṣṇu is eternal without a doubt, and it is also the abode of Mahā-Viṣṇu, the origin of all manifestations.
